Specimen information

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is impaired (elevated serum creatinine), and urinalysis shows significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible nephropathy and/or urinary tract pathology. HbA1c is low, indicating no chronic hyperglycemia. Electrolytes and other blood parameters are within normal limits. Recommend further evaluation for acute or chronic kidney disease and possible urinary tract infection or other urinary pathology.
